Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails using search_emails or list_emails (query: "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or similar) to find the full Vectral Q3 2026 contract renegotiation thread.
Search CRM using crm_search_deals (query: "Vectral" or similar) and crm_list_engagements (company_id: "company_vectral") to find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (stage: at_risk, no amount set, created April 18) and engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation.
Search emails using search_emails or list_emails (query: "NorthWind retention" or "retention model" or similar, or sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) to find Alejandro's working draft sent to Marcus containing three scenarios: Scenario A (hold current pricing - low credibility given recent events), Scenario B (12% Q3 discount on relocation services tied to full Denver expansion commitment), and Scenario C (10% discount + service guarantees - Alejandro's recommended approach).
Search emails using search_emails or list_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "PathFinder" or similar) to discover events that have occurred since Alejandro's retention draft was written, which affect whether the draft is still viable.
Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "vendor margin" or "Alejandro" or similar) to find two critical tickets: (1) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (#482, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations"), and (2) linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (#214, "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM" assigned to Alejandro Fuentes).
Search emails using search_emails or list_emails (query: "rate increase" or "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or similar) and search_emails (query: "pricing" or "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "DOT surcharge" or similar) to find two vendor rate change notices: (1) Jake Loomis's formal notice tied to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 invoice dispute; (2) Greg Pallone's immediate pricing change for Swift Relocations.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "vendor" or "rate" or "payment hold" or similar) across #finance (C005), #operations (C006), and #customer-engagement (C002) to find: Hana Kim's payment hold notice in #finance (breaking down the Heartland Q1 invoice), Elena Rostova's vendor strategy directive, and Marcus Thorne's vendor strategy memo marching orders to the team.
Search Linear using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170") to find the comment thread on Alejandro's ticket containing: Alejandro's initial anchoring note, David Chen's customer-engagement input request, Emeka Diallo's Mosaic exposure note, and David's follow-up noting that Catalina has not provided Midwest enterprise account input, leaving a blind spot in the client-impact analysis.
Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "Vectral" or "NorthWind" or "BrightLoop" or "Mosaic" or "Canopy" or "GreenStack" or similar) and quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Heartland" or "Swift" or similar) to find active billing data needed for margin impact analysis. Key invoices include: Vectral INV-2026-0410, BrightLoop INV-2026-0308, Canopy INV-2026-0401, and Heartland's disputed bill HM-2026-Q1-0042.
Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus" or "Thorne" or similar) to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the email write action.
Update Alejandro's open Linear ticket using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170") to update the description or status, and/or linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170") to add a status comment on issue #214 ("Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset"). The update should reflect current findings across all three workstreams - Vectral renewal awaiting board decision, NorthWind retention draft needing revision after April escalations, and vendor cost exposure from Heartland's 20% Q3 increase and Swift's 15% + $500/shipment surcharge - so the ticket is no longer stale.
Send email using send_email (sender: "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"]) with a consolidated briefing covering: (1) Vectral renewal - holding at 10% vs Rachel's 12% ask, total relationship value ~$109.9K at ~32% blended margin, awaiting board decision ~April 23; (2) NorthWind retention — draft scenarios A/B/C need significant revision given Pam Kowalski's formal final warning, PathFinder threat, Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, and Emilia Cruz piano damage claim ($1,200 KeyMove rider); (3) vendor cost exposure - Heartland 20% Q3 increase tied to unresolved $4,800 dispute, Swift 15% + $500/shipment effective immediately, with client-by-client margin impact across active contracts.
Post message using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005") in #finance with the key financial figures from the consolidated analysis: Vectral renewal value at stake (~$109.9K relationship, ~32% margin, discount negotiation pending), NorthWind retention exposure (Denver expansion deal $14,700, service failure costs accumulating), and vendor cost impact (Heartland 20% Q3 increase and Swift 15% + $500 surcharge hitting margins across active client contracts - BrightLoop, Mosaic, Canopy, GreenStack, and others all affected).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_deals, crm_search_companies, or crm_list_engagements).
tool selection

2 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_list_comments).
tool selection

6 Linear issue #214 ('Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM') was updated via linear_update_issue (id: 'linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170') or received a new comment via linear_create_comment (issueId: 'linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170').
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

7 The email body (visible in the send_email parameters) mentions the Vectral Systems renewal negotiation, stating that MoveOps is holding at a 10% loyalty discount — not 15% or 12% as the final approved offer.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The email body states 'we countered with the Preferred Partner Program at 10% discount' as MoveOps' position, and 'Rachel came back at 12%' as her counter. MoveOps' current position is clearly stated as 10%, not 15% or 12%.
✗Run #2The email does not mention MoveOps holding at a 10% loyalty discount. It states 'no counter-offer has been sent' and 'Mina is holding pending internal alignment.' The 10% approved counter-offer position is never mentioned — the email only references Rachel's 15% demand.
✗Run #3The email presents the negotiation as: Mina countered at 10%, Rachel came back at 12%. The email does NOT state MoveOps is holding firm at 10%. Instead it says 'We need to confirm whether Rachel accepted the 12% counter or if this is still open post-board meeting,' implying MoveOps may have moved to considering 12% rather than holding at 10%. The Slack post also frames 12% as Rachel's counter to Mina's 10% initial counter, but never asserts MoveOps' approved position remains 10%.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states 'Final offer sent to Rachel Whitfield: 10% loyalty discount on relocation services only' and 'David held firm at 10% after Rachel counter-proposed 12%'. MoveOps' position is clearly stated as 10%, not 12% or 15%.
✓Run #5The email body states 'Elena approved 10% on relocation services only' and 'David held firm at 10% — rejected Mina's request to go to 12%'. MoveOps' position is clearly identified as 10%, not 15% or 12%.
✓Run #6The email body states 'Our counter-offer draft: EST-2026-0419 proposes a 10% loyalty discount ($4,500 → $4,050/move)'. MoveOps' position is clearly stated as 10%, not 15% or 12%.
8 The email body or final response mentions that the Vectral renewal decision is pending a board presentation by Rachel Whitfield around April 23 (or similar deadline reference).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✗ 67%
